normalizedCandidates.searchIntent: active code lookup plus exact-name dispute cleanup
blockers: The 20Pearls and 20PEARLS exact-name dispute still needs a direct in-game or official code-box proof.
Broader public proof is still missing for whether 100DAYS, STRANDEDDEVS, or SEAADVENTURE were ever first-party expired names.

normalizedCandidates.phrase: 100 Days at Sea Trello Discord wiki

actionQueue.why: The current public packet still needs the CODE versus 20Pearls versus 20PEARLS dispute layer, and SuperCheats reopened tracker-only expired-name intent for 100DAYS, STRANDEDDEVS, and SEAADVENTURE.
Source-status intent remains covered on the existing route and did not need a same-run change.

sitemapNotes.note: The canonical codes route was updated to keep the CODE versus 20Pearls dispute live and to restore a clearly labeled tracker-only expired-name watch layer.

competitorDelta: Current code publishers are still split between CODE, 20Pearls, 20PEARLS, and 20 Pearls phrasing instead of converging on one exact input.
Pocket Tactics still keeps a no-live-codes contradiction visible, which strengthens the need for an explicit claim-state section on /codes.
SuperCheats is now surfacing 100DAYS, STRANDEDDEVS, and SEAADVENTURE as expired names, but today's broader collection did not repeat those terms elsewhere.

runSummary.primaryDecision: Update /codes and keep all code intent on the existing canonical route.
runSummary.rationale: The public code packet is still hot enough to justify visible exact-name dispute coverage, and the expired exact-name cluster returned on a public tracker, but neither change justifies a new route.

nextActionQueue.why: Re-check the live code-entry UI after the next official update or a same-build gameplay confirmation.
Promote direct Discord or Trello links only after a public creator-owned destination is verified.
